2605.21699.title: X-Token: Projection-Guided Cross-Tokenizer Knowledge Distillation
2605.21699.paper_summary: Cross-tokenizer knowledge distillation allows a student model to learn from teachers with incompatible vocabularies. Prior work operates on hidden states or logits; the latter is preferred as a drop-in replacement requiring no auxiliary components. Logit-based methods either use only the correct-token probability, missing the full 'dark knowledge' in the teacher's distribution, or operate on the full output distribution, relying on strict token partitioning and/or unprincipled heuristic ranking. We identify two key shortcomings of full-distribution, logit-based methods: (i) an uncommon-token failure, where critical tokens fall into the unmatched subset (e.g., Llama's 1100 multi-digit numerals under digit-splitting Qwen supervision) and are suppressed during training, reducing GSM8k from 12.89 to 2.56 compared to same-tokenizer KD from a weaker teacher; and (ii) over-conservative matching, where strict 1-to-1 matching excludes near-equivalent tokens across surface forms. These failures require distinct remedies: eliminating the partition when critical tokens are misaligned, and refining it when alignment is reliable. We propose X-Token, an approach with two complementary loss formulations targeting these issues. P-KL removes partitioning and aligns the student's distribution with the teacher's via a sparse projection matrix W (initialized from tokenizer-level string rules) to address the uncommon-token failure. H-KL retains the hybrid form while relaxing matching to align each student token with its top-ranked teacher mapping under W. Both objectives share W and extend naturally to multiple teachers. Empirically, on Llama-3.2-1B, X-Token outperforms the current state of the art GOLD by +3.82 average points with a Qwen3-4B teacher and by +0.5 with a Phi-4-Mini teacher. Further, a two-teacher setup (Phi-4-mini + Llama-3B) improves over single-teacher distillation by +1.3 points.
2605.21699.paper_summary_zh: 跨標記器知識蒸餾允許學生模型從具有不相容詞彙的教師學習。先前的工作在隱藏狀態或邏輯值上運作；後者更受青睞，因為它作為替代方案不需要額外的組件。基於邏輯值的方法要麼僅使用正確標記的概率，錯過了教師分佈中的完整「黑暗知識」，要麼在完整的輸出分佈上運作，依賴於嚴格的標記劃分和/或不具原則的啟發式排名。我們確定了基於完整分佈的邏輯值方法的兩個主要缺陷：（i）不常見標記失效，關鍵標記落入不匹配子集（例如，Llama 在數字分割 Qwen 監督下的 1100 個多位數字），並在訓練期間被抑制，將 GSM8k 從 12.89 降低到 2.56，相較於來自較弱教師的同標記器 KD；以及（ii）過於保守的匹配，嚴格的 1 對 1 匹配排除了表面形式間的近似等價標記。這些失敗需要不同的補救措施：在關鍵標記不對齊時消除劃分，並在對齊可靠時進行細化。我們提出了 X-Token，一種具有兩種互補損失公式的方案，針對這些問題。P-KL 消除了劃分，並通過稀疏投影矩陣 W（從標記器級字符串規則初始化）將學生的分佈與教師的分佈對齊，以解決不常見標記失效。H-KL 保留了混合形式，同時放寬匹配，將每個學生標記與其在 W 下排名最高的教師映射對齊。這兩個目標共享 W，並自然擴展到多個教師。在實證上，在 Llama-3.2-1B 上，X-Token 在使用 Qwen3-4B 教師時比當前最先進的 GOLD 提高了 +3.82 平均分數，使用 Phi-4-Mini 教師時提高了 +0.5。此外，兩教師設置（Phi-4-mini + Llama-3B）比單教師蒸餾提高了 +1.3 分。
2605.21699.author: Sharath Turuvekere Sreenivas et.al.
2605.21699.authors: Sharath Turuvekere Sreenivas, Adithyakrishna Venkatesh Hanasoge, Mingyu Yang, Ali Taghibakhshi, Saurav Muralidharan, Ashwath Aithal, Pavlo Molchanov
